speaker: EDWARD
text: I wonder how our princely father scaped,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Or whether he be scaped away or no

speaker: EDWARD
text: From Clifford’s and Northumberland’s pursuit.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Had he been ta’en, we should have heard the news;

speaker: EDWARD
text: Had he been slain, we should have heard the news;

speaker: EDWARD
text: Or had he scaped, methinks we should have heard

speaker: EDWARD
text: The happy tidings of his good escape.

speaker: EDWARD
text: How fares my brother? Why is he so sad?

speaker: RICHARD
text: I cannot joy until I be resolved

speaker: RICHARD
text: Where our right valiant father is become.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I saw him in the battle range about

speaker: RICHARD
text: And watched him how he singled Clifford forth.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Methought he bore him in the thickest troop

speaker: RICHARD
text: As doth a lion in a herd of neat,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or as a bear encompassed round with dogs,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Who having pinched a few and made them cry,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The rest stand all aloof and bark at him;

speaker: RICHARD
text: So fared our father with his enemies;

speaker: RICHARD
text: So fled his enemies my warlike father.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Methinks ’tis prize enough to be his son.

speaker: RICHARD
text: See how the morning opes her golden gates

speaker: RICHARD
text: And takes her farewell of the glorious sun.

speaker: RICHARD
text: How well resembles it the prime of youth,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Trimmed like a younker, prancing to his love!

speaker: EDWARD
text: Dazzle mine eyes, or do I see three suns?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Three glorious suns, each one a perfect sun,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Not separated with the racking clouds

speaker: RICHARD
text: But severed in a pale clear-shining sky.

speaker: RICHARD
text: See, see, they join, embrace, and seem to kiss,

speaker: RICHARD
text: As if they vowed some league inviolable.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Now are they but one lamp, one light, one sun;

speaker: RICHARD
text: In this, the heaven figures some event.

speaker: EDWARD
text: ’Tis wondrous strange, the like yet never heard of.

speaker: EDWARD
text: I think it cites us, brother, to the field,

speaker: EDWARD
text: That we, the sons of brave Plantagenet,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Each one already blazing by our meeds,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Should notwithstanding join our lights together

speaker: EDWARD
text: And overshine the earth, as this the world.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Whate’er it bodes, henceforward will I bear

speaker: EDWARD
text: Upon my target three fair shining suns.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nay, bear three daughters: by your leave I speak it,

speaker: RICHARD
text: You love the breeder better than the male.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But what art thou whose heavy looks foretell

speaker: RICHARD
text: Some dreadful story hanging on thy tongue?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Ah, one that was a woeful looker-on

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Whenas the noble Duke of York was slain,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Your princely father and my loving lord.

speaker: EDWARD
text: O, speak no more, for I have heard too much!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Say how he died, for I will hear it all.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Environèd he was with many foes,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And stood against them, as the hope of Troy

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Against the Greeks that would have entered Troy.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: But Hercules himself must yield to odds;

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And many strokes, though with a little axe,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Hews down and fells the hardest-timbered oak.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: By many hands your father was subdued,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: But only slaughtered by the ireful arm

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Of unrelenting Clifford and the Queen,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Who crowned the gracious duke in high despite,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Laughed in his face; and when with grief he wept,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The ruthless queen gave him to dry his cheeks

speaker: MESSENGER
text: A napkin steepèd in the harmless blood

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Of sweet young Rutland, by rough Clifford slain.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And after many scorns, many foul taunts,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: They took his head and on the gates of York

speaker: MESSENGER
text: They set the same, and there it doth remain,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The saddest spectacle that e’er I viewed.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Sweet Duke of York, our prop to lean upon,

speaker: EDWARD
text: Now thou art gone, we have no staff, no stay.

speaker: EDWARD
text: O Clifford, boist’rous Clifford, thou hast slain

speaker: EDWARD
text: The flower of Europe for his chivalry;

speaker: EDWARD
text: And treacherously hast thou vanquished him,

speaker: EDWARD
text: For hand to hand he would have vanquished thee.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Now my soul’s palace is become a prison;

speaker: EDWARD
text: Ah, would she break from hence, that this my body

speaker: EDWARD
text: Might in the ground be closèd up in rest,

speaker: EDWARD
text: For never henceforth shall I joy again.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Never, O never, shall I see more joy!

speaker: RICHARD
text: I cannot weep, for all my body’s moisture

speaker: RICHARD
text: Scarce serves to quench my furnace-burning heart;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nor can my tongue unload my heart’s great burden,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For selfsame wind that I should speak withal

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is kindling coals that fires all my breast

speaker: RICHARD
text: And burns me up with flames that tears would quench.

speaker: RICHARD
text: To weep is to make less the depth of grief:

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tears, then, for babes; blows and revenge for me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Richard, I bear thy name. I’ll venge thy death

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or die renownèd by attempting it.

speaker: EDWARD
text: His name that valiant duke hath left with thee;

speaker: EDWARD
text: His dukedom and his chair with me is left.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nay, if thou be that princely eagle’s bird,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Show thy descent by gazing ’gainst the sun;

speaker: RICHARD
text: For “chair” and “dukedom,” “throne” and “kingdom” say;

speaker: RICHARD
text: Either that is thine or else thou wert not his.

speaker: WARWICK
text: How now, fair lords? What fare, what news abroad?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Great lord of Warwick, if we should recount

speaker: RICHARD
text: Our baleful news, and at each word’s deliverance

speaker: RICHARD
text: Stab poniards in our flesh till all were told,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The words would add more anguish than the wounds.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O valiant lord, the Duke of York is slain.

speaker: EDWARD
text: O Warwick, Warwick, that Plantagenet

speaker: EDWARD
text: Which held thee dearly as his soul’s redemption

speaker: EDWARD
text: Is by the stern Lord Clifford done to death.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Ten days ago I drowned these news in tears.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And now to add more measure to your woes,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I come to tell you things sith then befall’n.

speaker: WARWICK
text: After the bloody fray at Wakefield fought,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Where your brave father breathed his latest gasp,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Tidings, as swiftly as the posts could run,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Were brought me of your loss and his depart.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I, then in London, keeper of the King,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Mustered my soldiers, gathered flocks of friends,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Marched toward Saint Albans to intercept the Queen,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Bearing the King in my behalf along;

speaker: WARWICK
text: For by my scouts I was advertisèd

speaker: WARWICK
text: That she was coming with a full intent

speaker: WARWICK
text: To dash our late decree in Parliament

speaker: WARWICK
text: Touching King Henry’s oath and your succession.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Short tale to make, we at Saint Albans met,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Our battles joined, and both sides fiercely fought.

speaker: WARWICK
text: But whether ’twas the coldness of the King,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Who looked full gently on his warlike queen,

speaker: WARWICK
text: That robbed my soldiers of their heated spleen,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Or whether ’twas report of her success

speaker: WARWICK
text: Or more than common fear of Clifford’s rigor,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Who thunders to his captives blood and death,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I cannot judge; but to conclude with truth,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Their weapons like to lightning came and went;

speaker: WARWICK
text: Our soldiers’, like the night owl’s lazy flight

speaker: WARWICK
text: Or like an idle thresher with a flail,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Fell gently down, as if they struck their friends.

speaker: WARWICK
text: I cheered them up with justice of our cause,

speaker: WARWICK
text: With promise of high pay and great rewards,

speaker: WARWICK
text: But all in vain; they had no heart to fight,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And we, in them, no hope to win the day,

speaker: WARWICK
text: So that we fled: the King unto the Queen;

speaker: WARWICK
text: Lord George your brother, Norfolk, and myself

speaker: WARWICK
text: In haste, posthaste, are come to join with you;

speaker: WARWICK
text: For in the Marches here we heard you were,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Making another head to fight again.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Where is the Duke of Norfolk, gentle Warwick?

speaker: EDWARD
text: And when came George from Burgundy to England?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Some six miles off the Duke is with the soldiers,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And, for your brother, he was lately sent

speaker: WARWICK
text: From your kind aunt, Duchess of Burgundy,

speaker: WARWICK
text: With aid of soldiers to this needful war.

speaker: RICHARD
text: ’Twas odds, belike, when valiant Warwick fled.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Oft have I heard his praises in pursuit,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But ne’er till now his scandal of retire.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Nor now my scandal, Richard, dost thou hear?

speaker: WARWICK
text: For thou shalt know this strong right hand of mine

speaker: WARWICK
text: Can pluck the diadem from faint Henry’s head

speaker: WARWICK
text: And wring the awful scepter from his fist,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Were he as famous and as bold in war

speaker: WARWICK
text: As he is famed for mildness, peace, and prayer.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I know it well, Lord Warwick; blame me not.

speaker: RICHARD
text: ’Tis love I bear thy glories make me speak.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But in this troublous time, what’s to be done?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Shall we go throw away our coats of steel

speaker: RICHARD
text: And wrap our bodies in black mourning gowns,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Numb’ring our Ave Marys with our beads?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or shall we on the helmets of our foes

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tell our devotion with revengeful arms?

speaker: RICHARD
text: If for the last, say “Ay,” and to it, lords.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why, therefore Warwick came to seek you out,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And therefore comes my brother Montague.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Attend me, lords: the proud insulting queen,

speaker: WARWICK
text: With Clifford and the haught Northumberland

speaker: WARWICK
text: And of their feather many more proud birds,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Have wrought the easy-melting king like wax.

speaker: WARWICK
text: He swore consent to your succession,

speaker: WARWICK
text: His oath enrollèd in the Parliament.

speaker: WARWICK
text: And now to London all the crew are gone

speaker: WARWICK
text: To frustrate both his oath and what beside

speaker: WARWICK
text: May make against the house of Lancaster.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Their power, I think, is thirty thousand strong.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Now, if the help of Norfolk and myself,

speaker: WARWICK
text: With all the friends that thou, brave Earl of March,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Amongst the loving Welshmen canst procure,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Will but amount to five and twenty thousand,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why, via, to London will we march,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And once again bestride our foaming steeds,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And once again cry “Charge!” upon our foes,

speaker: WARWICK
text: But never once again turn back and fly.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, now methinks I hear great Warwick speak.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ne’er may he live to see a sunshine day

speaker: RICHARD
text: That cries “Retire!” if Warwick bid him stay.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Lord Warwick, on thy shoulder will I lean,

speaker: EDWARD
text: And when thou fail’st—as God forbid the hour!—

speaker: EDWARD
text: Must Edward fall, which peril heaven forfend.

speaker: WARWICK
text: No longer Earl of March, but Duke of York;

speaker: WARWICK
text: The next degree is England’s royal throne:

speaker: WARWICK
text: For King of England shalt thou be proclaimed

speaker: WARWICK
text: In every borough as we pass along,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And he that throws not up his cap for joy

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shall for the fault make forfeit of his head.

speaker: WARWICK
text: King Edward, valiant Richard, Montague,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Stay we no longer dreaming of renown,

speaker: WARWICK
text: But sound the trumpets and about our task.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then, Clifford, were thy heart as hard as steel,

speaker: RICHARD
text: As thou hast shown it flinty by thy deeds,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I come to pierce it or to give thee mine.

speaker: EDWARD
text: Then strike up drums! God and Saint George for us!

speaker: WARWICK
text: How now, what news?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Duke of Norfolk sends you word by me,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Queen is coming with a puissant host,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And craves your company for speedy counsel.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Why, then it sorts. Brave warriors, let’s away!
